v2Native.reason: Deterministic deferred regression proving the post-listen orphan-worktree sweep settles before pool initialization can expose claimable entries, while unrelated request work remains responsive.

TypeScript AST/type-checker guard for the Async Background Cleanup goal: follows only the complete named preview artifact/mount/route/listener, worktree, plan-mutation, archive-purge, and orphan-cleanup production call graphs, and reports ASYNC_BACKGROUND_CLEANUP_SYNC_IO root-to-violation traces for direct, aliased, destructured, injected, or wrapped synchronous filesystem/subprocess/Atomics.wait operations.

Deterministic native v2 regression proving targeted Git cleanup cannot traverse a directory replaced by a symlink between lstat and open, remains idempotent for a missing target, and shares the process-wide recovery I/O concurrency ceiling across roots.

Deterministic native and memfs regressions proving bounded preview walks and removals reject real-directory identity replacement, whole-root preview installation quarantines a raced replacement without external traversal, and artifact metadata descriptors reject regular-file replacement before reading bytes.

Deterministic injected-filesystem regressions proving canonical preview and artifact roots stay identity-bound within trusted stores, opened canonical files match descriptor identities before reads, missing stable expectations fail closed, and whole-root staging substitution is quarantined without traversal or deletion.

Deterministic deferred regressions for source-ENOENT rollback, stable staging preflight, persistent failed-write fencing, same-key writer serialization, rename-first blocked purge, aborted stream lease release, and serialized owned-quarantine cleanup logging.

Deterministic native v2 coverage for coalesced immediate and expiry archive purges, stale-snapshot fencing, serialized preview purge cleanup, a single reclaim-candidate concurrency ceiling across many boot projects, and awaited CPU diagnostics shutdown.

Deterministic native v2 coverage for bounded deferred archive stats, event-loop progress, scheduled purge single-flight behavior, stop barriers that cancel future ticks and join active cleanup, and awaited failure-isolated termination listeners.

In-process gateway regression proving purge awaits the preview termination listener, the listener explicitly owns deletion errors without rejecting the purge response, retained artifacts remain targeted for later cleanup, and the gateway stays healthy.

Deterministic native v2 coverage for the shared background-I/O ceiling, bounded dynamic queues and traversal, cooperative deep-tree yielding, sorted files, streamed hashing input, symlink safety, bounded copy, and idempotent post-order deletion.
Unit coverage for atomic AIGW models/configuration failure behavior.

Pins installed-versus-source build detection and short commit SHA normalization for the Settings version label.
Exercises the running package version and build provenance endpoint against a real in-process gateway.

Pins the unit inventory audit's optional historical migration source policy with an injected Git reader: the exact absent-path-at-revision error returns empty text while unrelated Git failures remain fatal, without spawning a subprocess.

Pins the review-agent timeout resolver and lifecycle contract: shared 1200s default/1s minimum, explicit-shorter and agent-QA precedence, unchanged command defaults, fresh attempt/reminder/resurrection/restart windows, fixed operational settles, provider-backoff exclusion with 75s/330s grace, and session-only routing with the legacy direct path absent.

Integration coverage for machine-readable review timeout propagation through VerificationHarness start/complete events, failed GateStore persistence/reload, gate verification snapshots/counts, and WebSocket output sanitization without output string matching.

Chromium workflow-editor journey for type-aware command/llm-review/agent-qa timeout placeholders and help, explicit-shorter persistence, invalid-value normalization, and stale timeout removal when switching to human-signoff.

Deterministic Chromium journey using the real gate inspect/live renderers to pin structured timeout presentation, marker-derived timing, failed aggregate semantics, remediation validation, current/future/both scope routing, inherited-workflow customization ordering, active-snapshot isolation, and partial inline errors.
Native v2 unit coverage for injectable asynchronous model-completion commands, exact invocation options, parsing, failure mapping, timeout handling, and non-blocking deferred execution.

Pinning tests for buildResolvedModelStateModel (ws/handler.ts): custom/unknown provider (resolver source=inferred) preserves live contextWindow/maxTokens/reasoning/thinkingLevelMap, while known Fable model still gets authoritative catalog metadata incl. thinkingLevelMap.max over stale live base.

Reproducing test (TDD red) for the 'Fix skill autocomplete gap' goal (9e081770), Facet 2: scanSkillDir must discover nested Claude-plugin skills under BOTH a plugins-parent root (<dir>/<plugin>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md) and a plugin root (<dir>/skills/<name>/SKILL.md), keeping source=custom, while preserving the existing one-level <dir>/<name>/SKILL.md behaviour (no duplication). FAILS on HEAD (single-depth scan resolves zero nested skills), passes post-fix. Failure token SKILL_AUTOCOMPLETE_GAP.

Reproducing test (TDD red) for the 'Fix skill autocomplete gap' goal (9e081770), Facet 1: the Skills page's default slash-skills/details fetch must resolve against the currently-active session's project (state.activeProjectId), not Headquarters/system. Drives loadSkillsPageData with active project P and asserts the captured details URL carries projectId=proj-P and getConfigApiProjectId()==='proj-P'. FAILS on HEAD (scope defaults to system => Headquarters), passes once the page seeds scope from the active project. Failure token SKILL_AUTOCOMPLETE_GAP.

Regression test (spec requirement 2) for the 'Fix skill autocomplete gap' goal (9e081770): a REAL two-endpoint gateway test proving the Skills page (/api/slash-skills/details) and composer autocomplete (/api/slash-skills) resolve the IDENTICAL skill-name set for a given project. Registers project P (rootPath owns .claude/skills/only-in-p) and asserts both endpoints for P are set-equal AND include only-in-p; a second project Q proves scope isolation (Q's autocomplete must NOT surface P's skill). Replaces the tautological core test that called discoverSlashSkills twice with identical args and thus could not catch endpoint/parameter/projectId/config-store/market-context divergence.

Reproducing + decision-matrix tests for the destructive pre-verification goal-worktree sync: local-ahead un-pushed commit B must survive (not be discarded by `git reset --hard origin/<goalBranch>`), local-behind fast-forwards to origin, diverged keeps local + warns, up-to-date is a no-op. Drives the real VerificationHarness against real temp git repos.

Native v2 unit tests for the deletion-tombstones module (record/read round-trips, idempotency, per-file namespacing, dir creation, missing/corrupt-file tolerance) that guards staff/session/goal resurrection on boot repair.

Failing-first reproducing DOM test for Pause Feedback: no-descendant dashboard Pause, dashboard Resume, and transcript paused-banner Resume controls must immediately disable, show Pausing…/Resuming…, and suppress duplicate POSTs while the gateway request is pending.

Pinning DOM coverage for Create Goal proposal feedback: assistant preview and regular proposal panels must immediately show Creating…, disable/mark busy, suppress duplicate POST /api/goals calls while pending, and clear pending state after a failed create.
Failing-first reproducing tests for Permission Card UX reducer lifecycle: denied rows must remain as compact history, timed-out rows settle stale/expired, and superseded rows stop being actionable.

Failing-first reproducing DOM tests for Permission Card UX: blocked no-result tool calls must not render complete, streaming tool-call context must persist through tool_permission_needed, grant errors clear spinners, pinned+inline permission cards share state, pinned grant/deny settles rows, and pinned controls derive from current transcript rows.

Failing-first browser fixture for Permission Card UX: bottom-pinned active permission controls stay visible with a scrolled transcript, do not overlap editor/status/safe-area, grant/deny from pinned controls settle correctly, and reconnect/alive-socket remount derives pinned controls from current rows.

Reproducing test for the 'Fix cross-session proposal panels' goal: from a NON-matching session (assistantType === null) a role/tool/staff proposal that reaches the panel through the UNIFIED remote.onProposal path alone (source 'seed'/'rehydrate'/'edit'/'restore'/switch-back — not the live tool-use scan that also fires the legacy per-type callback) leaves the legacy form-mirror (rolePreviewName…, toolPreviewName…, staffPreviewName…) empty, so the panel renders blank and its primary submit stays disabled/absent. Drives onProposal directly (same technique as tests2/browser/e2e/proposal-inline-comments.spec.ts) and asserts the mirror is populated + submit enabled. FAILS on HEAD (no role/tool/staff bridge in the unified callback, only goal is bridged), passes once the bridge is added.

Failing-first browser journey for Project Proposal Accept no-op: registered Apply shows pending/disabled duplicate-click guard, registered rename failures surface a connection error without clearing the proposal, provisional config failures surface an error without cleanup, and registered success closes the proposal. Deterministic repro opts out of the retry bridge with retries:0. FAILS on HEAD until project proposal accept pending/error handling is fixed.

Browser regression coverage for Refresh Staff UI: while the app is open, staff create/delete calls made through API/tool-equivalent endpoints must update desktop and mobile sidebars without reload, show the staff row under Staff, and keep the permanent staff-agent session out of regular Sessions.

Reproducing test (TDD red) for the 'Fix git widget flash' goal (32cd6b4f): pins the NEW pure module src/app/git-repo-cache.ts — sessionId->'yes'|'no' localStorage cache and computeConnectGitState (cached 'no' => {gitRepoKnown:'no',quietRecheck:true} so no skeleton/loading on reconnect; cached 'yes'/no-entry => {gitRepoKnown:'unknown',quietRecheck:false} so a genuine first check still shows the skeleton), plus prune/cap bounds and broken/absent-localStorage tolerance. FAILS on HEAD (module absent — import cannot resolve), passes post-fix.

Integration coverage for the 'Fix git widget flash' goal (32cd6b4f): pins the session-manager quiet-recheck state machine extracted into runWidgetGitRefresh (src/app/git-status-refresh.ts). Asserts (1) a cached-'no' quiet reconnect NEVER writes gitStatusLoading=true, fires exactly one recheck, keeps gitRepoKnown='no' and caches 'no'; (2) when the quiet recheck's onOk fires the repo now exists so gitRepoKnown flips to 'yes', the widget reveals, cache writes 'yes', and loading is cleared in finally; (3) a genuine first-ever check (quiet=false / gitRepoKnown='unknown') DOES set gitStatusLoading=true (skeleton allowed) with one fetch.

Reproducing test (TDD red) for the 'Cache empty git-widget state' goal (44e3b434): pins the HQ 409 / GOAL_GIT_UNAVAILABLE path where git-status maps to error, retries exhaust with no branch/status data, and the widget renders nothing. Expects a persisted 'hidden' repo hint, cached-hidden reconnect to start quiet with no gitStatusLoading=true skeleton, exactly one quiet showable recheck, and reveal/cache 'yes' when content appears. FAILS on HEAD until the hidden/empty cache state is implemented.

Native v2 integration coverage for the 'Cross-project proposals' goal (96fddb01): the proposal seed endpoint resolves the TARGET project uniformly across goal/role/tool/staff — omitted → session project (system → headquarters), explicit valid cross-project accepted and stamped onto proposal.fields.projectId, explicit unknown → 422 UNKNOWN_PROJECT — while propose_project is exempt (a brand-new/unknown projectId is allowed at seed), and goal workflow validation resolves against the target project's workflows rather than the session's.

PR #1005 review (Greptile P1) coverage for the 'Cross-project proposals' goal (96fddb01): src/app/session-manager.ts::resolveProjectMode must derive a project proposal's provisional/registered mode from an EXPLICIT fields.projectId target when that target EXISTS (provisional target → 'provisional'/promote path; registered target → 'registered'/EDIT path), regardless of the source session's own mode. Only an unknown explicit target falls through to the source session, and an absent projectId keeps the source-session new-project flow. Pins the fix so a registered source targeting a provisional project no longer skips the required promote.

PR #1005 review (Greptile P1, 'Mode Stays Stale') coverage for the 'Cross-project proposals' goal (96fddb01): src/app/proposal-panels.ts::acceptProjectProposalFromPanel must recompute the registered-vs-provisional accept branch from the CURRENT fields.projectId via resolveProjectMode at dispatch time, not from the stale stored proposal.mode. Pins that a 'registered'-mode slot re-targeted to a provisional project takes the promote branch (POST /promote), a 'provisional'-mode slot targeting a registered project takes the EDIT branch (PUT /api/projects/:id, no promote), and a correct provisional slot still promotes.

Pinning test for the 'Direct-agent admin token' interim rollback: non-sandboxed (direct) agents receive the gateway ADMIN token (readToken()) via scopedGatewayEnvForDirectAgent (createSession/createDelegateSession) and applyScopedGatewayCredentials (restore/revive/respawn), while the sandbox path (mintScopedGatewayToken) still produces a per-project SCOPED token distinct from the admin token. Reproducing test for the 'Fix LLM review reliability' goal: pins per-attempt fresh reviewer session id (transcript not clobbered on retry) and honoring of a verification_result that arrives during teardown (no 404-drop). FAILS on HEAD, passes post-fix. Companion assertions pin the completed-but-missed-tool-call classification (non-transient → no from-scratch re-run) and that within-budget transient failures still retry.

Companion invariant for the 'Fix LLM review reliability' goal: SessionManager.createSession refuses to clobber an already-LIVE session id (loud throw), while opts.allowSessionReuse bypasses the guard for the sanctioned restart-resume path.
